voice.input_text: Hi, I'm Andrea. Welcome to BMH Group. This training is here to help you understand how we serve people, how the work moves, and what good judgment sounds like in a real conversation.

Most people who speak with us are trying to solve something. The property may need work. A move may be stalled. A family may be carrying a problem they did not expect. Start with the person and the situation before you start talking about a solution.

Ask clear questions. Listen all the way through the answer. A seller may mention price first, then reveal that timing, repairs, or a difficult family decision is the real concern. You cannot help with what you have not understood.

A cash, as-is path can remove some of the work that comes with preparing a property for a traditional sale. The exact price, timing, fees, and responsibilities depend on the written offer and the facts of that property. Explain only what has been confirmed. If you are unsure, pause and get the right answer.

The goal is not to push someone toward a yes. Help them understand the available path, answer what you can, and be honest about what still needs review. A clear no is more useful than a pressured maybe.

Good service also means doing what you said you would do. Keep the record current. Set the next action. Follow through, or tell the right person when something is blocked. Care and accountability belong together.
Continue into Mindset next. After that, take the Welcome and Mindset checkpoint and move into Terms Glossary.
